metadata.notes: agent_supports/daemon_capability/route are UNCHANGED pending a full re-assessment (see the 2026-07-11 addendum below and caveats) — driver_capability alone is updated: `/chat new` is now live-verified (kiro-cli 2.6.0) to mint a brand-new <uuid>.jsonl and drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh's reset_session arm is implemented and end-to-end tested (issue #935), superseding the prior 'UNVERIFIED' finding this cell's route was frozen on. A dedicated re-assessment pass is recommended to re-evaluate agent_supports/daemon_capability/route against this new evidence before attempting a recording.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready
route: frozen (pending re-assessment — see 2026-07-11 addendum)

## The scenario's user-observable signal

session-reset (1.5) asserts a session ROTATION: the old session row disappears and a NEW session row appears with a DIFFERENT `session_id` in `ready` (topology `ready → working → ready → ready → working → ready`). For claudecode and codex this is satisfied because their in-REPL reset (`/clear` / `/new`) materializes a brand-new transcript file with a fresh UUID — the daemon's FilesUnderRoot detects the new session_id and the #169 same-PID cleanup removes the old. kiro-cli does NOT behave this way via `/clear`.

## agent_supports: partial (as assessed 2026-06-05) — kiro-cli's `/clear` semantics DIVERGE

kiro-cli has reset commands, but the one verified by live smoke at that time (`/clear`) is an in-place CONTEXT wipe, not a session rotation:

- **`/clear` (VERIFIED, decisive)**: the SAME `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl` continues — same UUID, no new file. The only thing appended is a single `Clear` event (smoke session `c423461a…jsonl`: 4 Prompt / 7 AssistantMessage / 3 ToolResults / **1 Clear**, one UUID throughout). The embedded changelog confirms the intent: "Reset context manager on `/clear` so loaded skills revert to frontmatter-only" — it resets CONTEXT, not the session id. So NO new session_id appears and the old session does NOT disappear. The scenario's two core assertions are unmet by `/clear`.
- **`/chat new` — was UNVERIFIED as of 2026-06-05; NOW LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-07-11, kiro-cli 2.6.0) — see addendum below.**
- **`restart` (quit + relaunch)**: the only UNAMBIGUOUS new-UUID/new-PID path via `/clear`-era reasoning, but it is a fresh process launch, not an in-REPL reset — and the intervening process EXIT deletes the old session via process_exited (a process-death artifact, not the in-place old→new handoff the scenario models). It belongs to a launch scenario, not session-reset.

## daemon_capability: incapable (for the `/clear` path only) — and why it was NOT a bug

The daemon observes kiro-cli FAITHFULLY. `kirocli/agent.go:38` sets Source = FilesUnderRoot(~/.kiro/sessions/cli) — a new UUID-keyed file WOULD be detected as a fresh session_id exactly as it is for codex. The reason `/clear`'s signal is unobservable is that it emits NO rotation trace: the same file continues and the parser SKIPS the `Clear` event (`kirocli/parser.go:104-108`). With no new transcript file and no removal event, there is simply no trace for the daemon to surface via `/clear` — `incapable` per the contract ("no trace exists"), NOT a daemon defect.

## driver_capability: ready (updated 2026-07-11, issue #935)

`drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh`'s `reset_session` arm is no longer `not_implemented`: `step_reset_session()` sends `/chat new`, rewires the per-slot tmux pipe-pane capture, and eagerly verifies the new `<uuid>.jsonl` materialized before returning (propagated via `|| STEP_OK=false`). `DRIVE_ELICITS` now lists `reset_session`. End-to-end tested against the real `kiro-cli` binary (2.6.0): two distinct session_ids recorded, each slot's stdout correctly isolated, a guard added so a recipe targeting the retired old slot number cannot kill the live post-reset process (both slots alias the SAME tmux pane after a reset). See replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh's `step_reset_session` header comment for full timing-race rationale (kiro's `/chat new` materializes the new file SYNCHRONOUSLY, unlike codex/gemini's lazy resets — this changes the marker-vs-send ordering needed).

## 2026-07-11 addendum: `/chat new` is now LIVE-VERIFIED to rotate the session (issue #935)

While implementing the driver primitive above, `/chat new` was live-driven under tmux (kiro-cli 2.6.0, a fresh scratch cwd): sending `/chat new` after an established conversation (a) minted a brand-new `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid2>.jsonl` (plus `.json`/`.lock` sidecars) within ~1s, empty until the next turn; (b) the OLD `<uuid1>.jsonl` was never appended to again and its `.lock` was removed immediately; (c) a subsequent prompt correctly appended to the NEW file only. This is the exact rotation shape session-reset needs, and directly supersedes the `/chat new` UNVERIFIED caveat this cell's `frozen` route was resting on.

This addendum intentionally does NOT flip `agent_supports`/`daemon_capability`/`route` itself — those verdicts are the `assess` subagent's methodology (live daemon observation, not just on-disk artifacts), and this fix was scoped to the driver primitive. A dedicated re-assessment pass should re-run the `assess` methodology against this new evidence; based on the artifacts observed here, the likely outcome is `agent_supports: yes` / `daemon_capability: full` (the daemon's FilesUnderRoot mechanism was already argued, above, to detect exactly this shape) / `route: record`.

## What would fully unblock this cell

1. Run `/ir:onboarding-factory assess` (or equivalent) to re-evaluate `agent_supports`/`daemon_capability`/`route` against the 2026-07-11 addendum's live evidence.
2. If re-assessed to `record`, author the recipe (send → wait_turn → reset_session → send → wait_turn → exit_clean, mirroring the driver's own end-to-end test) and run a real `record` + `verify` pass to produce `expected.jsonl`.
caveats: DECISIVE evidence for `/clear` (live smoke 2.5.1, 2026-06-05): same UUID, same .jsonl, a single `Clear` event appended, parser skips it — no new session_id, no removal.

`/chat new`'s on-disk file behavior — UNVERIFIED as of 2026-06-05 — is NOW LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-07-11, kiro-cli 2.6.0, issue #935): mints a new <uuid>.jsonl, freezes the old file, removes its .lock. See the 2026-07-11 addendum in `body` for the full evidence trail.

daemon=incapable above is scenario-scoped to the `/clear` path and NOT an architectural defect: kiro's Source=FilesUnderRoot WOULD detect a new UUID file (as for codex) — this is exactly what was later observed for `/chat new`.

A full `restart` (quit + relaunch) is the only verified new-UUID/new-PID path via `/clear`-era reasoning, but it is a fresh process launch (old session removed via process_exited), not the in-place reset the scenario models — out of scope for session-reset.

driver_capability=ready reflects the DRIVER PRIMITIVE only (implemented + end-to-end tested against the real binary in this worktree, 2026-07-11). agent_supports/daemon_capability/route were deliberately left as originally assessed pending a dedicated re-assessment pass — flipping those is the `assess` subagent's methodology, not this driver fix's scope.

sources.note: §8 (2026-06-05): `/clear` continues the SAME session file (no new UUID, unlike claudecode); appends a Clear event. `/chat new` MAY start a new session (left UNVERIFIED at that time). restart mints a new UUID/PID but the process exit removes the old session.
Live 2.5.1 smoke (2026-06-05): ONE UUID throughout, 4 Prompt / 7 AssistantMessage / 3 ToolResults / exactly 1 Clear — `/clear` did not rotate the session id.
Lines 104-108: the `Clear` event is ev.Skip = true — it carries no state transition, so `/clear` emits no new session_id and no removal the daemon could surface.

Line 38: Source = FilesUnderRoot(~/.kiro/sessions/cli). A new UUID-keyed file WOULD be detected as a fresh session_id (as for codex) — confirming daemon=incapable (for /clear) is the absence of a trace, not a daemon defect.
2026-07-11 (issue #935): `reset_session` is now implemented (`step_reset_session`, sends `/chat new`) and listed in DRIVE_ELICITS; end-to-end tested against the real kiro-cli 2.6.0 binary.

Drove `/chat new` under tmux in a fresh scratch cwd after an established turn: a brand-new ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid2>.jsonl (+ .json/.lock) appeared within ~1s (empty until the next turn), the OLD <uuid1>.jsonl was never appended to again and its .lock was removed immediately, and a subsequent prompt correctly landed only in the new file. Supersedes the prior 'UNVERIFIED' finding.

Embedded changelog (2026-06-05 smoke): 'Reset context manager on /clear so loaded skills revert to frontmatter-only' (context reset, not session rotation) and 'Added /chat new command in V2 TUI to start a fresh conversation without restarting the CLI' — the on-disk file behavior this predicted is what the 2026-07-11 live verification confirmed.

preconditions: The driver primitive is now READY (issue #935, 2026-07-11): drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh's reset_session sends /chat new and is end-to-end tested against the real binary.

Still not recorded: a full re-assessment (agent_supports/daemon_capability/route) and an authored recipe + a real `record`/`verify` pass are needed before this cell can move off `frozen`. See the assessment body's 2026-07-11 addendum and 'What would fully unblock this cell' section.
